title: Lesson 2.1 — Setting Up Your OpenRouter Account

steps.caption: Step 1: Go to OpenRouter.ai
steps.desc: OpenRouter is the gateway to every major AI model — GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, and more.

steps.caption: Step 1: OpenRouter Homepage
steps.desc: OpenRouter is the service that powers your AI agents. Think of it as the electricity for your agents — without it, they don't run. The good news: getting set up is free and takes about 3 minutes. Click 'Sign In' in the top right.

steps.caption: Step 2: Sign Up Page
steps.desc: Click 'Sign Up' and create your account. You can use Google, GitHub, or your email. Once signed in, you'll be taken to your dashboard. We need one thing from here: your API key.

steps.caption: Step 3: Get Your API Key
steps.desc: Navigate to the API keys section.

steps.caption: Step 3: Your API Keys Dashboard
steps.desc: This is what your API Keys page looks like once you have keys. Each key has a name, usage tracker, and spending limit. To create your first one: click '+ New Key' in the top right. Give it a name like 'My First Agent'. You'll see a key starting with sk-or-v1-... — COPY IT NOW and save it in a safe place (notes app or password manager). You only see it once. Notice how you can set a spending limit per key — great for keeping costs under control while you're learning.

steps.caption: Step 4: Browse the Available Models
steps.desc: See which AI models you can use with your key.

steps.caption: Step 4: All the AI Models Available to You
steps.desc: With your OpenRouter key, you have access to ALL of these models. GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, Gemini, Llama, Mistral — everything. Many have free tiers. We'll start with GPT-4o Mini for your agent — it's fast, cheap, and excellent for customer conversations. You can switch models any time without changing your agent's code.

steps.caption: Step 5: Head to the Agent Widget
steps.desc: You have your OpenRouter key. Now let's see exactly where to put it. Go to the Alexander AI Agent Widget — this is where you'll create and deploy your AI agent.

steps.caption: Step 5: The Agent Widget Platform
steps.desc: This is the platform where you'll build and deploy your AI agent. Sign up for free, then click 'Create New Agent' to get started. In the next lesson we'll write the system prompt — but first you need to know where your OpenRouter key goes.

steps.caption: Step 6: Open the New Agent Form
steps.desc: Click 'Create New Agent' to open the agent creation form.

steps.caption: Step 6: This Is Where Your API Key Goes
steps.desc: See the 'OpenRouter API Key' field? This is where you paste the key you just copied from OpenRouter. It starts with sk-or-v1-... Paste it here and the Widget is powered. Your agent will use this key every time a customer sends a message — you pay OpenRouter pennies per conversation, and you control the spending limit.

steps.caption: Step 7: Key In — You're Connected
steps.desc: When your key is pasted in, it looks like this. The field shows your key (masked for security). Once you fill in this field and hit Create, your agent is connected to the world's best AI models. The hard part is done — in Lesson 2.2, we'll write the system prompt that makes your agent actually useful.
